codeReview.summary: The mem-episodic-memory feature was already fully implemented in event_log.rs prior to this session. The worker correctly identified that only the test module rename from 'tests' to 'episodic' was needed to enable test discovery via 'cargo test -p swell-memory -- episodic'. The implementation covers all required event types (tool_invocation, decision, observation, error, outcome) with immutable timestamped EventLogEntry storage in append-only JSONL format.

issues.description: ObservationPayload type and EventType::Observation variant exist (line 28, 117-122), but there is no corresponding log_observation() method on EventLog struct. While the feature description mentions 'observation', the other event types (tool_invocation, decision, error, outcome) all have dedicated log_*() convenience methods. For consistency, a log_observation() method could be added.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill documentation doesn't mention any specific procedure for handling features that are already implemented. The worker correctly identified the situation and performed only the minimal change needed (renaming the test module). No skill gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, no deviations noted in handoff

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention issues observed. The worker followed the AGENTS.md conventions for Rust (async_trait, thiserror, tracing, etc.) and properly exported the event_log module via pub mod event_log in lib.rs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib.rs line 13: 'pub mod event_log;' - properly exported

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services or commands were needed. The verification commands in AGENTS.md (cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-memory) were sufficient.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification section shows cargo build and cargo test commands were run successfully

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that cargo test filters work on module names, and renamed 'mod tests' to 'mod episodic' to enable test discovery via 'cargo test -p swell-memory -- episodic'. This is a useful pattern that could be documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit message and transcript show this discovery during verification

summary: Review passed. The mem-episodic-memory feature implementation in event_log.rs is complete with all required event types (tool_invocation, decision, observation, error, outcome) stored as immutable timestamped EventLogEntry in append-only JSONL format. The worker correctly identified the feature was already implemented and only needed to rename the test module from 'tests' to 'episodic' for test discovery. One non-blocking observation: a log_observation() convenience method could be added for consistency with other event types, though the ObservationPayload type already exists and could be used directly.
